conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first check the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your inquiry! I'll help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To assess this properly, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child, Alex, who's 6 years old and lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few more questions.

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, this affects eligibility for Child Benefit.

Could you tell me which country you're currently living in? Also, are you or have you been a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of HM Forces posted overseas)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm living in Spain at the moment. And no, I'm not a Crown servant - I'm not a diplomat or in the armed forces or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since you're living in Spain and are not a Crown servant, I need to ask a few more questions to determine eligibility.

Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Based on the information you've provided, I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarize your situation:
- You have one child, Alex, who is 6 years old and lives with you
- You are currently living in Spain
- You are not a Crown servant (diplomat or member of HM Forces)
- You do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme

According to the UK government rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and **you live in the UK**. There are limited exceptions for people living abroad - specifically if you're a Crown servant or living in certain countries under specific circumstances related to the EU Settlement Scheme.

Unfortunately, based on your circumstances, you would not be eligible for Child Benefit while living in Spain without Crown servant status or EU Settlement Scheme status.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry, but based on the assessment, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex** at this time.

The main reason is that you don't currently meet the UK residency requirement. To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally need to live in the UK. While there are some exceptions for people living abroad, these apply to Crown servants or those with settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, which don't apply in your situation.

If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if you move back to the UK - you would be able to claim Child Benefit at that time, as Alex meets the age requirement and lives with you.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Child is not in local authority care

Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old and lives with the claimant, which would normally meet the age and responsibility requirements. However, the claimant is living in Spain and does not meet any of the exceptions for living abroad. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. The claimant may be able to get Child Benefit if they are a Crown servant or living in certain countries with settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. The claimant is not a Crown servant and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Therefore, the claimant does not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex (age 6), who lives with them. While the child meets the age requirement, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirement as they live in Spain. They do not qualify for any of the exceptions for living abroad (Crown servant status or EU Settlement Scheme status), and therefore are not eligible for Child Benefit.

They do not qualify for any of the exceptions for living abroad (Crown servant status or EU Settlement Scheme status), and therefore are not eligible for Child Benefit.
